meta.title: Seedwarden Phase 2 — Lifestyle Photography Production Checklist

items.task: Record baseline Etsy Stats for all live listings (views, conversion rate, revenue, favorites)
items.output: Baseline data logged in WORKLOG.md or analytics spreadsheet

items.task: Identify any listing with 20+ views and under 2% conversion rate — flag as high-priority photo target
items.output: Priority adjustment list (modifies default Tier ranking if needed)

items.task: Verify which Phase 1 listings are live and searchable (blocked listings cannot have slots 4-5 added)
items.note: Native Plants Regional Guide PDF rebuild may still be pending at this point

items.task: Confirm $80-$160 budget available for Phase 2 execution
Make go/no-go decision: proceed with Phase 2 photography on Week 4 schedule
items.no_go_condition: If Phase 1 shows near-zero conversions across all listings after 3 weeks, defer Phase 2 and investigate root cause (keyword mismatch, pricing, description gaps) before spending photography budget

pre_phase2_preparation.label: Pre-Phase-2 Preparation (run in parallel with Phase 1, before decision gate)

items.task: Photographer briefing package finalized and ready to send
If contracting external photographer: send briefing package, secure shoot date in their calendar
items.note: Photographers need 2-3 week lead time — book before Phase 1 data arrives

items.task: Review props list against what is on hand; identify gaps
props_needed: Germination tray or egg carton with starter mix
Small garden trowel or hand fork
3-5 mason jars (at least 1 with visible contents)
Wooden cutting board (11x15 inch or larger)
2-4 small potted plants (herbs or small houseplants)
Tablet (charged, screen brightness max, all PDF covers staged)

items.task: Determine shooting location for each cluster

locations_needed.cluster_a: Wooden surface near window (kitchen table, workshop, outdoor potting bench)
locations_needed.cluster_b: Window sill, balcony, or indoor plant shelf with urban context visible
locations_needed.cluster_c: Kitchen counter or wooden table with mason jar setup

items.task: Identify a 3-4 hour morning window (9am-2pm) in Week 5 for the physical shoot
Begin free stock image search for 6 Cluster D/E products (can start immediately, no approval needed)

week_4.label: Week 4 — Stock Images for 6 Cluster D/E Products

items.task: Search free sources (Unsplash, Pexels, Pixabay) for all 6 Cluster D/E products
items.output: 3-5 candidate images per product saved to staging folder

items.task: End-of-Day-1 gap assessment: identify products where free sources returned no usable image
items.decision: If 5-6 products have gaps: subscribe iStock Essential ($40/month) for 1 month. If 3-4 gaps: buy on-demand credits ($12-15/image). If 0-2 gaps: skip iStock entirely.
istock_priority_order: Small-Scale Livestock Field Manual (highest gap risk)
Hunting/Fishing/Trapping Manual (trapping imagery not on free sites)
Meat/Fish Preservation Field Manual (artisan smokehouse gap)
Survival Garden Regional Plans (regional specificity gap)
Native Plants Regional Guide (likely $0 iStock spend)

items.task: Purchase iStock credits for confirmed gaps only (on-demand, not subscription, unless 5+ gaps)
items.note: Do not pre-purchase. Buy only after free-source gap assessment is complete.

items.task: Log every sourced image in WORKLOG.md: product name, source URL, license type, filename
items.required_for: Wikimedia CC BY-SA images require attribution in listing description footer

items.task: Composite each stock image with product PDF cover overlay (Canva or Photoshop)
items.technique: Stock image as background; product PDF cover appears as printed page resting on surface in the scene, or as tablet display in mid-ground

items.task: Save all composited images to naming convention
QA: verify 12 images exist (2 per product x 6 products), spot-check 4 at full resolution
qa_criteria: Product identifiable in at least one of the two images

items.task: Upload Cluster D/E lifestyle images to Etsy slots 4-5 for all 6 products
items.note: Do not upload Native Plants if listing is still blocked by PDF file size issue. Store images in etsy-ready/ folder for immediate upload when listing goes live.
items.output: Record upload date in WORKLOG.md — conversion measurement window starts from this date

week_5.label: Week 5 — Physical Photography for 15 Cluster A/B/C Products

items.task: Props gathering (complete 2 days before shoot, not morning-of)
Pre-load all 15 product PDF covers on tablet; confirm screen brightness at maximum
Shoot Day 1: Cluster A (8 products — Seeds/Garden)

schedule.action: Cluster A setup (wooden surface, window, props out)
Shoot all 8 Cluster A products (8 min each: 1 flat-lay + 1 contextual per product)

items.note: Print Companion Planting Chart and Zone Calendar for flat-lay shots — tablet is less effective for these two products

items.task: Shoot Day 1: Cluster B (4 products — Container/Urban Growing)

schedule.action: Cluster B setup (move to window sill or balcony, add potted plants)
Shoot all 4 Cluster B products (10 min each)

items.task: Shoot Day 1 or Day 2: Cluster C (3 products — Food Preservation)

schedule.action: Cluster C setup (kitchen counter, mason jars, cutting board, peppers)
Shoot all 3 Cluster C products (10 min each)

items.safety_note: Wear gloves or wash hands after handling high-capsaicin peppers before touching eyes

items.task: Capture bonus social media assets during shoot (portrait/vertical frames for Instagram/TikTok, wide landscape for Pinterest/Etsy banner)
bonus_capture_targets: 15-second video of hands packing mason jar (Cluster C — TikTok B-roll)
10-second video of seeds poured from envelope (Cluster A — TikTok B-roll)
1 wide establishing shot per cluster setup (for Pinterest 2:3 pin or Etsy banner)

items.task: Editing Day: import photos, edit first image per cluster, batch-apply settings
File size check: run all exports through Squoosh (squoosh.app) — confirm each under 1MB
Review all exports at 100% zoom; flag images for reshoot
flag_criteria: Product not clearly visible in either shot
Harsh shadows cutting across surface or product
Background too cluttered to read on-theme
Lighting too dark (exposure cannot be recovered without visible noise)

items.task: Reshoot buffer: reshoot only flagged images (typically 2-4 images maximum)
items.fallback: If physical photo fails for any product, use stock image fallback from Unsplash/Pexels — $0 cost, 30 minutes per product

week_6.label: Week 6 — Final Compositing, QA, and Etsy Upload

items.task: Compositing: for any physical photo where product is not literally in frame, overlay PDF cover as flat printed-page in Canva
items.note: 5 minutes per image; only needed for images where product is implied but not visible. Not needed if tablet was in the shot.

items.task: Final QA — all 42 images

technical: 42 images total (2 per product x 21 products) — count and verify
All files sRGB color space — verify
All files named by naming convention — verify
etsy_compliance: No text overlay that duplicates the listing title
No watermarks or photographer branding visible in frame
No competing brand logos or product labels visible
No imagery that implies a physical product will be shipped
conversion_criteria: Product identifiable in at least one of the two images per product
Props support the product theme — tell the same story as the product title
Lighting soft and even — no harsh shadows across the product
Hands (if present) look working — unpainted, mid-task
Background clean enough not to distract; busy on-theme backgrounds are acceptable
brand_consistency: No shot reads as aspirational or wealthy-homestead aesthetic
Earthy and practical tone across all images
licensing: Every stock image logged in WORKLOG.md with source URL and license type
Wikimedia CC BY-SA images have attribution noted for listing description footer
iStock images have image ID and standard license confirmation logged

items.task: Etsy upload: add lifestyle images to slots 4 and 5 for all remaining 15 Cluster A/B/C listings
instructions: Open each listing in Etsy Seller Dashboard
Add slot 4 image (flat-lay or lifestyle shot)
Add slot 5 image (in-use or contextual shot)
Verify thumbnail crop in 1:1 preview shows product clearly
Do not change any other listing element during this upload — isolate the photography variable

items.task: Record upload date for each listing in WORKLOG.md
items.note: The 30-day conversion measurement window starts from the upload date, not from the beginning of Phase 2

items.task: Verify all 21 listings are at 5-image status (slots 1-5 complete)
items.exception: Native Plants Regional Guide may still be at 3-image status if PDF rebuild is not complete — this is acceptable; do not block the rest of Phase 2

conversion_measurement.label: 30-Day Conversion Measurement Window
conversion_measurement.timing: Week 10 post-Phase-1-launch (30 days after last upload)

items.task: Day 7 check-in: scan for any change in favorites rate or sales directly referencing listings
Day 14 interim read: are views trending up or flat? Any conversion rate shift for Cluster D/E (uploaded in Week 4)?
Day 30 full comparison: calculate percentage change per metric for all 4 high-ticket products
metrics_to_compare: Views per listing (30-day post vs. 30-day pre)
items.success_threshold: 10-20% conversion rate lift is 'strong signal'. Under 5% lift across all products: evaluate whether Etsy Ads or Pinterest promoted pins are a better next investment than further photography.

items.task: Decision: if conversion lift is 10%+, prioritize Phase 3 social media content using best-performing lifestyle images as visual anchors

Decision: if conversion rate lift is under 5% across all 4 products after 30 days, investigate alternative levers (Etsy Ads, description improvements, pricing) before additional photography investment

line_items.item: iStock credits — Small-Scale Livestock Field Manual
line_items.notes: 2 images if free sources fail; highest gap risk product
2 images; trapping imagery not available on free sites

line_items.item: iStock credits — Meat/Fish Preservation Field Manual
line_items.notes: 0-2 images; some artisan charcuterie content on free sites

line_items.item: iStock credits — Survival Garden Regional Plans
line_items.notes: 0-1 image; generic raised-bed content available free

line_items.item: iStock credits — Native Plants Regional Guide
line_items.notes: Wikimedia Commons primary source; likely $0 iStock spend
Skip entirely if window light is adequate. Check before purchasing.
Most should be on hand or sourced locally; $30 covers any gaps

line_items.item: Printed product pages for flat-lay shots
line_items.notes: Color printing at library or print shop; only needed for Companion Planting Chart and Zone Calendar
Stays within $160 ceiling; lower end achievable if free sources cover most Cluster D/E products

contingencies.mitigation: Execute physical shoot independently using DIY setup from PHOTOGRAPHY_ROADMAP.md Section 5. Shot lists are identical. Phone camera with window light is sufficient for all 15 physical products.
contingencies.reschedule_window: 1-week delay (shoot in Week 6 instead of Week 5) does not materially change Phase 2 outcomes — Cluster D/E stock images go live in Week 4 regardless

contingencies.risk: Physical shoot produces unusable images (poor lighting, cluttered background, product not legible)
contingencies.mitigation: Use reshoot buffer (W5-10). For any product where physical photo fails entirely, fall back to stock: Unsplash/Pexels cover all Cluster A/B/C themes. Fallback cost: $0. Fallback time: 30 minutes per product.
contingencies.decision_rule: A mediocre physical shot should be replaced with a strong stock image rather than uploaded. Quality threshold applies equally to all images.

contingencies.risk: iStock budget runs out before all Cluster D/E gaps are filled
contingencies.mitigation: Execute strict priority order: Livestock Manual and Hunting/Trapping get iStock credits first. If budget runs out, Survival Garden and Native Plants have adequate free-stock coverage. Launch without the last 1-2 images rather than overspend.
contingencies.decision_rule: Never delay the entire Phase 2 upload because one product does not have lifestyle photos. Upload the products that are ready.

contingencies.mitigation: Test compositing approach on one product before committing to all 12 stock images. If the overlay looks obviously digital (wrong perspective, mismatched lighting), switch to a flat 2D overlay in the corner of the stock image, clearly labeled 'Digital Download'. This is less immersive but still communicates context.

contingencies.risk: Phase 1 conversion data suggests lifestyle photos are low priority
contingencies.mitigation: If Phase 1 shows listings converting at 3%+, lifestyle photography is still worth doing but no longer urgent. Defer to Month 2. If Phase 1 shows near-zero conversions across all listings, defer Phase 2 entirely and investigate root causes (keyword mismatch, pricing, descriptions) before spending photography budget.
contingencies.trigger_for_deferral: Under 5 total purchases across all listings after 3 weeks live

contingencies.risk: Native Plants Regional Guide still blocked (PDF exceeds Etsy 5MB limit)
contingencies.mitigation: Source and prepare stock images for this product on schedule, but do not upload slots 4-5 until the listing is active. Store composited images in etsy-ready/ folder. Upload immediately when the PDF rebuild is complete and the listing goes live.

qa_gate.label: What Constitutes an 'Approved Photo' for Etsy Upload
qa_gate.description: An image must pass all items in each category below before it is uploaded to Etsy. An image that fails any single item is either replaced from alternates, re-edited, or reshot.

etsy_compliance: No text overlay duplicating the listing title
No competing brand logos or product labels in frame
No imagery implying a physical product will be shipped
conversion_quality: Product identifiable (tablet screen, printed page, or clearly implied) in at least one of the two slot images
Props are thematically appropriate to the product
Lighting is soft and even — no harsh shadows across the product or surface
Hands (if present) look working — unpainted, mid-task
Background is clean enough to read as intentional; on-theme busy backgrounds acceptable
brand_consistency: Warm color temperature (5500-6000K range) — no cool or blue cast
Earthy and practical tone — not aspirational or polished
No Pottery Barn aesthetic (no pristine white kitchen, no perfect produce on marble)
licensing: Stock image source URL and license type logged in WORKLOG.md
Wikimedia CC BY-SA images: attribution text noted for listing description footer
iStock images: image ID and standard license confirmation logged
